level_signals: 5+ years of progressive experience in Product Management or Product Development
Compensation range $90,000–$100,000 CAD, consistent with mid-senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of progressive experience in Product Management or Product Development, ideally within a food manufacturing, D2C subscription, or FMCG environment.
A customer-centric mindset: you have the ability to identify unmet customer needs and pain points; a knack for creative thinking to develop solutions that solve our customers' challenges.

Outstanding ability to manage meetings, present to diverse audiences, and shift perspectives to negotiate and secure genuine buy-in for strategic initiatives across international teams and cross-functional departments.
Exceptional ability to break down complex, ambiguous problems into manageable focus areas and build data-driven business cases.
Deep understanding of product discovery and delivery frameworks with a proven track record of applying them to a physical supply chain.

A strong entrepreneurial streak and a desire to excel in a high-growth, unstructured, and fast-paced environment. You are passionate about creating the future and shaping the ready-to-heat category in Canada.
Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Business, or a related technical field (e.g., Food Science).

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of progressive experience in Product Management or Product Development, ideally within a food manufacturing, D2C subscription, or FMCG environment.

scope.surface: Factor Canada product portfolio — menu assortment, positioning, pricing, and ready-to-heat meal category
core_responsibilities: Define and own key aspects of the product roadmap
Serve as the definitive authority on the customer using quantitative and qualitative insights
Act as primary champion for the customer across trade-off decisions
Lead product discovery for new concepts
Partner with Culinary, Operations, and other stakeholders in co-creating solutions
Digest and summarize product launches, A/B tests, or other data sets into actionable insights
Manage commercial viability of the portfolio (quality, cost trade-offs)
Collaborate with international brand counterparts to share best practices
Take a rapid testing approach to learn and pivot quickly

green_flags: Clear product ownership area — Factor Canada menu portfolio
Explicit hybrid policy (3 days on-site) stated upfront
Role reports directly to Director of Product — clear reporting line
Benefits package detailed and competitive (health from day 1, RRSP matching, generous PTO)
red_flags: Compensation stated in CAD only — no USD equivalent; role is Canada-based and likely not open to US candidates
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international applicants
Physical product / food manufacturing domain is niche — limits applicant pool from pure software PM backgrounds
Disclosure that allergens are present in the workplace may be a concern for some candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from 'D2C subscription' and Factor Canada product description)
compensation.base_range_usd (not stated; range is in CAD — field left null, custom base_range_cad added for accuracy)
ambiguities: The 'ideally within a food manufacturing, D2C subscription, or FMCG environment' qualifier on the 5+ years requirement is ambiguous — the industry context reads as preferred ('ideally') but the years of experience itself is unconditional. Industry background captured as a separate nice-to-have; years of experience treated as hard.
Compensation is listed in CAD; schema expects USD. base_range_usd left null; a non-schema field base_range_cad [90000, 100000] noted in this field.
